description_long: This sculpture is a bronze vessel with a round, deep bowl set atop a square pedestal base, reminiscent of ancient Chinese ritual bronzes. The bowl features intricate relief patterns and two prominent handles shaped like stylized animal heads, possibly dragons or mythical creatures, extending outward and upward. The surface has a weathered, greenish patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The square base is also decorated with geometric motifs and appears solid and heavy. The overall form is symmetrical, with the handles and decorative motifs providing a sense of balance and grandeur. The vessel's open top and substantial size indicate it may have served as a ceremonial or decorative object.
description: A weathered bronze vessel with ornate animal-head handles, set on a square pedestal and covered in intricate relief patterns. The sculpture has a greenish patina and an open, bowl-like top.
description_view_0: Front view showing one of the prominent animal-head handles and the detailed relief patterns on the bowl and base.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both handles in full, revealing their symmetrical placement and the open bowl's depth.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the opposite handle and matching decorative motifs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing both handles and the vessel's round shape atop the square base.
attribute_subject_matter: ancient ritual vessel with animal motifs
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
